rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical trifluoromethylation/iminyl-radical-mediated 1,5-HAT cyclization of N-cyanamide alkenes with CF3SO2Na to give trifluoromethylated bicyclic amidines and N-acryloylpyrrolidin-2-one derivatives
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na (2a, Eox = 1.13 V vs SCE) to generate CF3 radical (after loss of SO2); a second anodic oxidation converts the post-1,5-HAT benzylic radical IIIa to the benzylic carbocation IVa, which cyclizes to product. Cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered radical Ia from CF3• addition to N-cyanamide alkene 1a
iminyl radical IIa from intramolecular cyclization of Ia
benzylic radical IIIa formed by 1,5-HAT from iminyl radical
benzylic carbocation IVa from anodic oxidation of IIIa

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2- to CF3SO2• which extrudes SO2 to give CF3•; CF3• adds to the N-cyanamide alkene to form C-radical Ia that cyclizes onto the cyanamide N≡C to give iminyl radical IIa; 1,5-HAT generates benzylic radical IIIa, which is further oxidized at the anode to benzylic cation IVa; intramolecular cyclization of the iminyl nitrogen onto the cation and deprotonation furnishes amidine 3a, while H+ is reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a not detected, consistent with radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: 3a not detected; CF3-DPE adduct 8 isolated in 7% yield, trapping CF3 radical

probe_reagents.name: water (acetone/H2O 5/1)
probe_reagents.observation: Isolation of α-hydroxy pyrrolidinone 4 (21%) supports benzylic cation intermediate IVa

probe_reagents.name: HFIP cosolvent (acetone/HFIP/H2O 10/1/1)
probe_reagents.observation: α,α-Diphenylacetyl-pyrrolidin-2-one 5 (18%) isolated, supporting iminyl radical intermediate IIa

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 2a/1a/3a/6c/7c
probe_reagents.observation: 2a oxidizes at 1.13 V, well below 1a (2.43 V), 6c (2.32 V), 3a (2.50 V), and 7c (1.46 V), indicating preferential anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na and explaining overoxidation susceptibility of 7

notes: Two parallel product branches from common radical intermediates: bicyclic amidine 3a (from cation IVa cyclization) and N-acryloylpyrrolidinone 7a (from elimination/hydrolysis of cation IVb derived from substrate 6a). Side product 4 (water trap of IVa) and 5 (from iminyl radical IIa pathway) serve as diagnostic intermediates rather than productive intermediates. No oxidant or transition metal required; two-step anodic oxidation distinguishes this from Cu-catalyzed oxidative addition/reductive elimination pathway.
